hero.title: 12 Days, Srinagar to Leh.
hero.titleAccent: The Greatest Road Trip in the Himalayas.
hero.intro: One of the most spectacular journeys on earth. Drive from the green valley of Kashmir over high mountain passes to the moonscapes of Ladakh, taking in turquoise lakes, ancient monasteries and the roof of the world.

breadcrumb.label: 12 Days Srinagar to Leh Road Trip

hero.heroImageAlt: Srinagar to Leh Ladakh Himalayan road trip

overview.heading: 12 Days Srinagar to Leh Road Trip: The Full Picture
body: The Srinagar to Leh road trip is a journey through some of the most dramatic landscapes on the planet. You begin in the lush Kashmir valley, then climb over high passes into Ladakh, a high-altitude desert of stark mountains, turquoise lakes and centuries-old Buddhist monasteries. It is an adventure of a lifetime.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, this 12 day road trip covers Srinagar, Sonamarg, Kargil, the Lamayuru and Alchi monasteries, Leh, the Nubra Valley with its sand dunes and camels, and the unforgettable Pangong Lake. Your own vehicle and experienced driver guide handle the high mountain roads, with time to acclimatise to the altitude along the way.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Pangong Lake
items.body: The astonishing high-altitude lake that shifts through shades of blue and turquoise, set against barren mountains at over 4,300 metres. One of the most beautiful sights in the Himalayas.

items.title: Ladakh's Monasteries
items.body: Ancient Buddhist gompas like Thiksey, Hemis, Lamayuru and Alchi cling to the mountains, full of prayer flags, murals and chanting monks. The spiritual heart of Ladakh.

items.title: Nubra Valley & High Passes
items.body: Cross some of the world's highest motorable passes to reach the Nubra Valley, where sand dunes and double-humped Bactrian camels sit beneath snow peaks.

days.title: Arrive Srinagar, Houseboat
days.description: Met at Srinagar airport and transferred to your Dal Lake houseboat. Evening shikara ride.

days.title: Srinagar Sightseeing
days.description: The Mughal gardens and old city.

days.title: Srinagar to Kargil
days.description: Drive over Zoji La pass via Sonamarg and Drass to Kargil.

days.title: Kargil to Leh
days.description: Drive to Leh via the dramatic Lamayuru and Alchi monasteries.

days.title: Leh Acclimatisation & Local Sights
days.description: A rest day to acclimatise to the altitude, with gentle local sightseeing.

days.title: Leh Monasteries
days.description: The great monasteries of the Indus valley.

days.title: Leh to Nubra Valley
days.description: Cross Khardung La, one of the highest motorable passes, to the Nubra Valley.

days.title: Nubra to Pangong Lake
days.description: Drive across to the spectacular Pangong Lake.

days.title: Pangong to Leh
days.description: Sunrise at the lake, then return to Leh over Chang La pass.

days.title: Leh Leisure & Shopping
days.description: A relaxed day in Leh for markets and any missed sights.

days.title: Leh Free Day
days.description: A buffer day to relax or explore further, useful given the high altitude and mountain weather.

days.title: Depart Leh
days.description: Checkout and transfer to Leh airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: The Himalayan Road Trip, Done Safely
whyBook.body: High passes, altitude and remote roads make Srinagar to Leh a trip that demands experience. We build in acclimatisation, use seasoned drivers, and assist with permits so the adventure stays safe. Dhesu has arranged Himalayan journeys for many years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Srinagar to Leh Road Trip from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 12 day road trip is one of the great Himalayan adventures, crossing from the green Kashmir valley over high passes into the stark, beautiful high-altitude desert of Ladakh.

The route takes in Pangong Lake, the Nubra Valley, ancient monasteries and some of the highest motorable passes in the world. It is a journey for travellers seeking dramatic landscapes and a real sense of adventure.

We build in acclimatisation days and use experienced mountain drivers to keep the trip safe and comfortable.

sections.heading: Altitude and the Best Time to Go
sections.body:
Ladakh sits at very high altitude, with Leh around 3,500 metres and the passes far higher. Acclimatisation is essential, which is why this itinerary includes rest days, and we strongly advise taking the altitude seriously.

The Srinagar to Leh road is open roughly from June to September, when the passes are clear of snow. This is the only window for the full road trip, so book well ahead for these months.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Srinagar and Leh Airports (SXR / IXL). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: When is the Srinagar to Leh road open?
items.answer: The road is generally open from June to September, when the high passes are clear of snow. Outside this window the route is closed by snow, so the full road trip can only run in these summer months. Book early for the best arrangements.

items.question: How serious is the altitude?
items.answer: Very. Leh sits around 3,500 metres and the passes are much higher. Altitude sickness is a real risk, so this itinerary includes acclimatisation days. We advise consulting your doctor before travelling and taking the altitude seriously throughout.

items.question: Do I need permits for Ladakh?
items.answer: Yes. Inner Line Permits are required for areas like Nubra Valley and Pangong Lake. We assist with arranging these as part of the package. You will need passport copies and photos, which we will request before your trip.
